response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and distributed systems engineer with strong testing culture and some large-scale infrastructure exposure, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position's hard requirement of 8 years of C++ experience is essentially unmet — C++ does not appear in his listed skills, and the only identifiable C++ work is a ~6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis project. His career trajectory is firmly rooted in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember.js), not cloud compute platform engineering in C++, and there is no evidence of production-level cloud architecture ownership or technical leadership of cloud/DevOps teams.
strengths: Demonstrated large-scale distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-performance design
Strong, consistent testing culture across the entire career — TDD advocacy, open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and hands-on workshop facilitation
Staff-level roles and community mentoring (recurring Refactoring Dojos, multi-module Selenium courses, public talks) show genuine leadership and knowledge-sharing capacity
concerns: C++ is absent from his skills list and his verifiable C++ experience totals roughly 6 months — a critical gap against the 8-year minimum requirement
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes at a side project do not constitute production cloud architecture leadership
Career is web/full-stack oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang); there is no systems-programming or hardware-adjacent work that would bridge toward compute platform engineering
